System Design

Rate Limiter

Rate Limiter

Here’s the figure showing rate limiting algorithms:

  • Fixed Window Counter: Counts requests in a fixed time window.
  • Sliding Log: Stores timestamps of recent requests.
  • Sliding Window Counter: Counts requests in a moving time window.
  • Token Bucket: Uses tokens that refill over time.

✅ Comparison Table

Algorithm

How It Works

Pros

Cons

Fixed Window Counter

Count requests in fixed intervals

Simple, predictable reset

Allows bursts at window boundaries

Sliding Log

Store timestamps for each request

Accurate

High memory usage for large traffic

Sliding Window Counter

Approximate sliding window count

Smooth rate limiting

Slightly complex, still approximate

Token Bucket

Tokens refill at fixed rate

Allows bursts, smooth control

More complex to implement

Copilot said:

Copilot

Here are real-world use cases for each rate limiting algorithm:

✅ Fixed Window Counter

  • Use Case: Simple API rate limiting
  • Example:
    • Allow 100 requests per minute per user.
    • Common in internal APIs or low-traffic services where burstiness is acceptable.

✅ Sliding Log

  • Use Case: High-accuracy rate limiting for critical services
  • Example:
    • Payment gateways or financial APIs where exceeding limits could cause fraud.
    • Stores exact timestamps for precise enforcement.

✅ Sliding Window Counter

  • Use Case: Public APIs with smooth traffic control
  • Example:
    • Social media APIs (Twitter, Facebook).
    • Prevents bursts while allowing flexibility.

✅ Token Bucket

  • Use Case: Streaming and real-time services
  • Example:
    • Video streaming platforms (Netflix, YouTube).
    • Allows bursts for buffering but enforces overall rate.

Here’s the diagram showing rate limiting algorithms with real-world use cases:

  • Fixed Window Counter → Simple API rate limiting
  • Sliding Log → Financial APIs (high accuracy)
  • Sliding Window Counter → Social media APIs (smooth traffic control)
  • Token Bucket → Streaming services (allows bursts for buffering)
